audit_items.proof_trace: Two error return paths leak heap memory: at L421 (when `db = calloc(...)` returns NULL), `flist` is returned without being freed; at L428 (when `db->buf == NULL || db->fname == NULL`), both `flist` and `db` are returned without being freed. The sole `free(flist)` call is on the success path at L444 only, and no cleanup block exists before any error return.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker or low-memory condition that triggers `calloc`/`strdup` failure during proxy startup causes `flist` (and `db`) to leak; repeated restarts or OOM conditions at initialization will accumulate leaked memory, and the missing free before error return is a confirmed bug with no intentional design justification.
